# VariScout

> Structured investigation for process improvement. Browser-based, customer-owned data, local-cache capable. PWA (public, free) + Azure Marketplace tiers (Standard €79/mo, Team €199/mo).

Entry points for AI agents working in this repo. Humans should start at `docs/index.md`.

## Core orientation

- [decision-log.md](decision-log.md): living decision tracker — open questions, named-future features, session backlog, journey state. Read this before re-opening any topic; it captures decisions that would otherwise live only in plan files.
- [OVERVIEW.md](OVERVIEW.md): what VariScout does, the FRAME → SCOUT → INVESTIGATE → IMPROVE journey, six analysis modes, three distribution tiers.
- [USER-JOURNEYS.md](USER-JOURNEYS.md): ten personas, unified journey spine, process learning levels, and analysis-mode experiences.
- [DATA-FLOW.md](DATA-FLOW.md): parse → transform → stats → persist → sync → display → AI pipeline. Three numeric-safety boundaries.
- [superpowers/specs/2026-04-27-product-method-roadmap-design.md](superpowers/specs/2026-04-27-product-method-roadmap-design.md): product-method maturity roadmap across Process Hub, Current Process State, Process Measurement System, process learning memory, and CoScout context.
- [superpowers/specs/2026-04-27-process-learning-operating-model-design.md](superpowers/specs/2026-04-27-process-learning-operating-model-design.md): process learning operating model; Process Measurement System, Current Process State, response paths, and the three-level outcome / flow / local-mechanism methodology (faithful to the existing Y / X / x EDA spine).
- [superpowers/specs/2026-04-26-evidence-sources-data-profiles-design.md](superpowers/specs/2026-04-26-evidence-sources-data-profiles-design.md): canonical Evidence Sources, Data Profiles, Snapshots, and Profile Applications design.
- [superpowers/specs/2026-04-27-layered-process-view-design.md](superpowers/specs/2026-04-27-layered-process-view-design.md): three-band visual language (Outcome / Process Flow / Operations) for FRAME, Process Hub, and multi-hub.
- [superpowers/specs/2026-04-29-investigation-scope-and-drill-semantics-design.md](superpowers/specs/2026-04-29-investigation-scope-and-drill-semantics-design.md): investigation scope (B1/B2 unified), drill patterns (Hub→Step shipped; Step→Channels existing; Step→Sub-flow + Org Hub-of-Hubs designed), governance, and the no-statistical-roll-up policy.
- [superpowers/specs/2026-04-29-multi-level-scout-design.md](superpowers/specs/2026-04-29-multi-level-scout-design.md): multi-level SCOUT — level-spanning surface architecture (L1 outcome / L2 flow / L3 mechanism lensed across SCOUT, FRAME, Hub Capability, Investigation Wall, Evidence Map) plus the timeline-window primitive; boundary policy at [07-decisions/adr-074-scout-level-spanning-surface-boundary-policy.md](07-decisions/adr-074-scout-level-spanning-surface-boundary-policy.md).
- [07-decisions/adr-076-frame-b0-lightweight-render.md](07-decisions/adr-076-frame-b0-lightweight-render.md): FRAME branches on scope — investigator (b0, lightweight Y/X picker) vs author (b1/b2, river-styled SIPOC canvas — ADR-070). `detectScopeFromMap(map)` is the dispatch point. No auto-pick, no upfront warnings in b0.
- [01-vision/trainer-network.md](01-vision/trainer-network.md): lightweight trainer ecosystem model for VariScout growth, certification, and directory routing.

## Architecture

- [Decisions (ADRs, live)](07-decisions/): current architectural decisions. Start at `07-decisions/index.md`.
- [Decisions (archived)](archive/adrs/): superseded decisions preserved for traceability.
- [Designs (specs)](superpowers/specs/): active design work. Start at `superpowers/specs/index.md`.

## Agent-specific context

- `../AGENTS.md`: Codex entrypoint for root project instructions and Codex-specific ruflo usage.
- `../CLAUDE.md`: Claude entrypoint for root project instructions and Claude-only automation.
- `../packages/*/CLAUDE.md` and `../apps/*/CLAUDE.md`: per-package context, loaded on demand.
- `../.claude/rules/`: path-scoped rules that load automatically when editing specific code areas (stats, charts, CoScout prompts, Azure storage, tests, i18n).
- `../.claude/skills/`: task-triggered workflow skills.
- `05-technical/implementation/ruflo-workflow.md`: shared ruflo workflow across agent clients.
- `05-technical/implementation/codex-ruflo-workflow.md`: Codex-specific MCP and workflow notes.

## Reference

- [Timeline Windows in Investigations](03-features/analysis/timeline-window-investigations.md): user-facing reference for the four window kinds and where the picker lives.
- [Multi-Level Dashboard](03-features/analysis/multi-level-dashboard.md): user-facing reference for how SCOUT spans levels and links to Hub Capability / Evidence Map / Investigation Wall.
- [Timeline Window & Multi-Level Routing Architecture](05-technical/architecture/timeline-window-architecture.md): engineer-facing reference for `dataRouter`, `detectScope`, TimelineWindow, throughput, drift detection.
- [Glossary](03-features/learning/glossary.md): domain terms, statistical vocabulary.
- [Feature parity matrix](08-products/feature-parity.md): PWA vs Azure Standard vs Azure Team.
- [Statistics reference](05-technical/statistics-reference.md): formulas, NIST validation, citations.

## FRAME b0 lightweight render (ADR-076)

Priority entry points for the b0 (investigator archetype) FRAME surface — paste data → ColumnMapping → Y/X picker → "See the data →".

- `../packages/ui/src/components/FrameViewB0/`: b0 lightweight composition — stacks Y picker, optional inline spec editor, X picker, process-steps disclosure expander, and the "See the data →" CTA.
- `../packages/ui/src/components/ColumnCandidateChip/`: chip primitive shared by Y and X picker sections.
- `../packages/ui/src/components/YPickerSection/`: Y picker — surfaces ranked Y candidates from `rankYCandidates`; "+ add spec" affordance opens the inline editor.
- `../packages/ui/src/components/XPickerSection/`: X picker — appears after Y is selected; multi-select; auto-detected run-order column hidden.
- `../packages/ui/src/components/InlineSpecEditor/`: popover-style spec editor anchored under the Y row; edits USL / LSL / target / cpkTarget; suggestion seeded from mean ± 3σ.
- `../packages/ui/src/components/ProcessStepsExpander/`: disclosure that embeds the existing `LayeredProcessViewWithCapability` canvas with `showGaps={false}`. Adding the first step flips scope b0 → b2 and the canvas takes over.
- `../packages/ui/src/components/SeeTheDataCta/`: primary CTA to advance to the Analysis tab; disabled until a Y is selected.
- `../packages/core/src/parser/yLikelihood.ts`: `rankYCandidates` heuristic — 16 case-insensitive name patterns + rich-variation bonus, capped; never auto-picks.
- `../packages/core/src/scopeDetection.ts`: `detectScopeFromMap(map)` — b0/b1/b2 dispatch from `ProcessMap.nodes.length`. Mirrors `detectScope(investigation)` for surfaces that have a map but no `nodeMappings` yet (PWA + Azure FRAME entry).

## Tooling

- `pnpm docs:check`: runs diagram health, orphan detection, dead-link scan, Ruflo version drift, frontmatter schema validation.
- `pnpm codex:ruflo-check`: verifies active Codex Ruflo registration/version and prints repair commands if missing, disabled, or stale.
- `scripts/audit-doc-references.mjs`: reference-map tool for load-bearing audits (see `docs/09-baseline/2026-04-17-doc-audit.md`).
- Frontmatter schema: `scripts/docs-frontmatter-schema.mjs` (warn mode until 2026-05-15).
